patterns: UK councils use .gov.uk abbreviations (hullcc, rbwm, tmbc, lbhf, rbkc, pkc, rctcbc, bathnes, dumgal)
Northern Ireland councils use .com/.org TLDs (derrystrabane.com, fermanaghomagh.com, midulstercouncil.org)
Scottish Gaelic: cne-siar.gov.uk = Comhairle nan Eilean Siar (Western Isles)
Irish county councils use abbreviations (dlrcoco, sdcc)
Portuguese Câmara Municipal abbreviations: cm-oaz.pt, cm-sjm.pt, cmpv.pt, cmvfc.pt, cm-vrsa.pt, cm-fcr.pt, cmav.pt, cmpb.pt, cm-sbras.pt, cm-gaia.pt
MCR = Município de Caldas da Rainha

categories: altanet.org subdomains: 61 municipalities — regional IT platform, not municipal domains
blogspot.com/blogia.com: 11 — blog platforms used as municipality 'websites'
free hosting (weebly.com, webcindario.com, netne.net, galeon.com, localtic.net): 10
wrong_patterns.note: These domains should NOT be used for email classification — they belong to hosting platforms, not municipalities. Most have no MX or belong to the platform.

correct_patterns.note: Most flags are low-risk (score 20-35) abbreviation mismatches — Spanish municipality names with 'de/del/la/las/los' prepositions that get stripped from domain slugs. These are legitimate.

ES.action: ES needs dedicated seed data cleanup for the ~87 platform domains. These should have domain cleared or corrected to actual municipal domain (.es/.gob.es). A separate script could detect platform hosting patterns automatically.
